production.playableLoop: Move the player across the arena to reach the goal feedback.
production.objective: Trigger visible and audio feedback by reaching the goal zone.
production.progression: The starter remains a single objective slice; expand it with score, timer, waves, or collectible count before claiming a complete generated game.
production.failRetry: Reset by rebuilding or reloading the preview; no persistent fail state is authored in this starter.
feedbackMoments: Player movement is visible in playtest proof.
Goal feedback changes scene state when the player reaches the objective.
HUD text can be extended from GameState bindings.

assetPlan.playerHero: Starter player entity is source-owned placeholder geometry intended to be replaced by catalog, kit, or authored custom art.
assetPlan.obstacleEnemy: No obstacle/enemy is authored in the starter baseline.
assetPlan.rewardInteractable: Goal zone is the starter reward/interactable surface.
assetPlan.worldEnvironment: Arena floor, camera, light, and goal context provide the starter world surface.
assetPlan.uiHud: Starter HUD/source UI is the baseline for objective and state text.
assetPlan.audioFeedback: Starter goal-ping audio asset is available for feedback wiring.

highValueSurfaces.summary: Starter player entity is source-owned placeholder geometry intended to be replaced by catalog, kit, or authored custom art.
No obstacle/enemy is authored in the starter baseline.
Goal zone is the starter reward/interactable surface.
Arena floor, camera, light, and goal context provide the starter world surface.
Starter HUD/source UI is the baseline for objective and state text.
Starter goal-ping audio asset is available for feedback wiring.

uiStates.expectation: Starter records retry limitation before a full fail state is authored.

assetSourcing: Starter assets are local source-owned baseline surfaces; run tn asset source search --format glb --direct-only --json before replacing high-value art.
knownBlockers: Starter player and world surfaces are intentionally minimal until a generated game goal selects art direction.
